summary:
Cabozantinib is an effectively used second-line treatment for advanced hepatocellar carcinoma after progressive failure on sorafenib, supported by FDA-approved guidelines and documented efficacy in delaying disease progression and prolonging survival. It acts through dual pathway inhibition targeting both vascular endothelial growth factor and c-MET signaling—key mechanisms driving liver cancer aggressivity—and has been consistently included across multiple international standards-of-care recommendations. 

The summary above contains redundant content not strictly necessary for answering the query; please remove any repetition while preserving only essential facts related to the core medical point asked about.

Correct Summary:

Cabozantinib is clinically effective as a second-line therapy for advanced hepatocellular carcinoma following sorafenib resistance, per established guidelines and mechanistic rationale based on targeted suppression of angiogenesis and tumorigenic signals.  
It demonstrates improved overall survival compared to placebo/standard care in pivotal trials and remains recommended within global oncology consensus frameworks.
